message:
release: keep release.yml on OIDC; add scripts/claim-npm-names.sh

Reverting the NPM_TOKEN detour. release.yml stays OIDC-only with
`--provenance`, matching the turboquant-wasm pattern.

Adds scripts/claim-npm-names.sh for the one-time `publish then
configure trust` setup the npm UI requires. The script publishes a
v0.0.0 of each of the six names from temp dirs, lists the per-package
Access URLs to configure OIDC trust on, and prints the tag command
once trust is wired up.

Use:
  npm login
  bash scripts/claim-npm-names.sh
  # configure Trusted Publisher per package on npmjs.com
  git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0

files.patch:
@@ -166,9 +166,8 @@ jobs:
       # npm trusted publishing (OIDC). The job's `id-token: write`
       # permission lets `npm publish --provenance` exchange the GitHub
       # OIDC token for an npm publish token at request time — no
-      # NPM_TOKEN secret needed. Configure on npmjs.com under each
-      # package's "Trusted Publishers" → GitHub Actions, pointing at
-      # this repo + this workflow file.
+      # NPM_TOKEN secret needed. Configure on npmjs.com per package
+      # before tagging.
       - name: Publish per-triple subpackages
         run: node scripts/publish-natives.mjs
         env:

@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# One-time: publish a v0.0.0 of each of the 6 npm names so they exist
+# in the registry. npm's per-package Trusted Publisher UI only appears
+# on packages that already exist; once these v0.0.0 versions are up,
+# the OIDC config can be added per package on npmjs.com, and the real
+# v0.1.0 release goes out via CI without needing NPM_TOKEN.
+#
+# Run as the npm user that should own all 6 names (i.e. `teamch`).
+#
+# Usage:
+#   npm login                   # if not already logged in as `teamch`
+#   bash scripts/claim-npm-names.sh
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+NAMES=(
+  textsift
+  textsift-linux-x64
+  textsift-linux-arm64
+  textsift-darwin-x64
+  textsift-darwin-arm64
+  textsift-win32-x64
+)
+
+WHOAMI="$(npm whoami)"
+if [[ "$WHOAMI" != "teamch" ]]; then
+  echo "expected npm user 'teamch', got '$WHOAMI'" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+WORKDIR="$(mktemp -d)"
+trap 'rm -rf "$WORKDIR"' EXIT
+
+for name in "${NAMES[@]}"; do
+  echo
+  echo "==> publish $name@0.0.0"
+  pkgdir="$WORKDIR/$name"
+  mkdir -p "$pkgdir"
+
+  cat > "$pkgdir/package.json" <<EOF
+{
+  "name": "$name",
+  "version": "0.0.0",
+  "description": "See https://github.com/teamchong/textsift",
+  "license": "Apache-2.0",
+  "repository": { "type": "git", "url": "git+https://github.com/teamchong/textsift.git" },
+  "homepage": "https://github.com/teamchong/textsift"
+}
+EOF
+
+  ( cd "$pkgdir" && npm publish --access public )
+done
+
+echo
+echo "All 6 names published at v0.0.0. Next steps:"
+echo
+echo "  1. Visit each package on npmjs.com and configure Trusted Publisher"
+echo "     (type GitHub Actions, repo teamchong/textsift, workflow release.yml):"
+for name in "${NAMES[@]}"; do
+  echo "     https://www.npmjs.com/package/$name/access"
+done
+echo
+echo "  2. Once all 6 have OIDC configured, tag the real release:"
+echo "     git tag v0.1.0 && git push origin v0.1.0"
